name: Guan Gu
page_title: Guan Gu 觀鵠 – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes:
Guan Gu commanded a religious bandit group under the title General of the Peaceful Heaven (or General Who Pacifies Heaven). When he died in AD 187, Zhou Chao and Guo Shi, likely his successors, ravaged Lingling, Guiyang, and Changsha until they were sought out and destroyed by Sun Jian, Administrator of Changsha, later in the same year. (1)

(1): Dr. Rafe de Crespigny

full_text:
Guan Gu 觀鵠
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Guan Gu (觀鵠)was born in Lingling. Commanded the religious bandit group which, after he died, passed to Guo Shi and Zhou Chao. Guan Gu lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Guan Gu commanded a religious bandit group under the title General of the Peaceful Heaven (or General Who Pacifies Heaven). When he died in AD 187, Zhou Chao and Guo Shi, likely his successors, ravaged Lingling, Guiyang, and Changsha until they were sought out and destroyed by Sun Jian, Administrator of Changsha, later in the same year. (1)
(1): Dr. Rafe de Crespigny
